extra.reasoning: The input `rash` appears only in r4 (priority 100), which outputs `self_care` — the LOWEST acuity. Setting rash=true can only activate r4, which, due to its overwhelming priority, overrides every other rule. This breaks monotonicity. Counterexample baseline: severe_headache=T, palpitations=T, back_pain=T, slurred_speech=T, rash=F, vision_loss=F. With rash=F: r2 (palpitations, prio 3) and r5 (slurred_speech, prio 3) both fire to emergent; r3/r6 fire to urgent (prio 2); r4 does not fire. Highest-priority match is prio 3 → acuity = emergent. Now flip rash to true (all else unchanged): all five conjuncts of r4 (severe_headache, palpitations, back_pain, slurred_speech, rash) are true, so r4 fires at priority 100, dominating everything → acuity = self_care. Acuity drops from emergent to self_care, i.e., setting rash=true LOWERS acuity, violating the property.
